title: Release v0.5.38 after Work Item 1
outOfScope: Changing release workflow implementation or enterprise-security boundary.
Claiming provider publication before the release workflow produces assets.

sources.reason: Public release projection to promote after WI1 execution.
Candidate projection for the next release.
Canonical release state and source identity.
Record WI1 impact and release sequencing evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Release preparation is fail-closed; provider publication remains a separate post-merge verification.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized execution of WI1, its impact assessment, and a new release after the corrected serial sequence.

preReviewWarnings: Verify exact source identity, candidate/public projection separation, generated evidence, and bounded claims.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: v0.5.38 is prepared from the latest main after WI1 and the budget repair are merged.
Release projections, canonical state, and generated evidence are internally consistent.
The release workflow publishes the exact source-bound tag and provider assets after merge.
The recalibration plan records WI1 impact and the new release evidence without claiming unavailable assets.
All declared AI and project quality checks pass.
guidelines: Release claims must be bound to independently verifiable source and artifact evidence.
Do not claim provider publication or enterprise security beyond available evidence.

intent.problem: After correcting the sequence, WI1 must be executed and its impact reconciled before publishing a new release; v0.5.37 remains immutable historical evidence.
constraints: Use the latest origin/main after merged WI1 and budget repair.
Keep one Work Item, one branch, and one PR.
Publish only after PR merge and verify tag, assets, smoke, and compatibility evidence.
intent.rationale: A fresh release Work Item makes the corrected ordering auditable and binds the new version to the post-WI1 main commit.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Public and candidate release projections remain distinct and agree with canonical state.
The release is bound to the post-WI1 default-branch commit and generated evidence.
Provider assets and security claims remain bounded until workflow evidence exists.

rawUserRequest: Execute Work Item 1 now, assess and reconcile its impact on the whole plan, then publish a new version; do not skip Work Item 1.

declaredIntent.summary: Publish a new source-bound release after executing WI1 and reconciling the complete plan.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the independent budget repair and continuation through WI1 impact reconciliation and the new release.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
